summary: Implement the 1985-NY-Yankees Azure Function: a nightly Timer Trigger (2 AM UTC) that queries GPT-4o via TRAPI for the 1985 New York Yankees roster, validates the response, and persists structured JSON to a dedicated Azure Blob Storage account using Managed Identity for all service authentication. Covers infrastructure provisioning via Bicep, application code in Python 3.11, CI/CD via GitHub Actions with staging slot swap, observability via Application Insights, and Key Vault-based secret management.
assumptions: TRAPI supports Azure AD Managed Identity bearer tokens; if not, a Key Vault-stored credential will be used as fallback.
The GPT-4o model version to pin will be determined during TRAPI confirmation (PRJ-01) before APP-02 implementation begins.
The 1985 roster scope is limited to the 25-man active roster (24–28 players) until PRJ-01 confirms otherwise.
TRAPI is accessible over the public internet; no VNet integration is required for initial deployment.
Consumption Plan SLA (~99.95%) is acceptable; Premium Plan is not required unless a strict 2 AM SLA is confirmed.
The existing Bicep modules (infra/) will be extended rather than replaced.
GitHub Actions secrets (AZURE_CREDENTIALS, AZURE_SUBSCRIPTION_ID) are already configured or will be set up as part of REL-01.
Application Insights sampling settings already present in host.json are preserved and extended.
Python 3.11 runtime and azure-functions v2 programming model are used throughout.
The dedicated storage account is separate from the Function App host storage account.
open_questions: TRAPI auth mechanism: Does TRAPI support Azure AD Managed Identity bearer tokens, or is a client credential / API key required? (Owner: rciapala — blocks APP-02)
TRAPI endpoint and API version: What is the base URL and API version for the GPT-4o endpoint? (Required before APP-02)
GPT-4o model version to pin for deterministic output: Which exact model version string should be used in the prompt template?
Player scope: Active 25-man roster only, or include coaching staff, front-office, and injured list? (Affects prompt template and validation thresholds)
Networking: Is TRAPI network-restricted, requiring VNet integration and private endpoints for storage?
Consumption Plan vs. Premium Plan: Is there a strict SLA on the 2 AM execution window that requires always-ready instances to avoid cold-start risk?

waves.name: Foundation
waves.objective: Resolve blocking external dependencies, provision all cloud infrastructure via Bicep, and approve the implementation task plan before any application code is written.
entry_criteria: spec.md reviewed and implementation task plan generated
Azure subscription and resource group available
GitHub repository access confirmed for team
exit_criteria: PRJ-01: TRAPI auth mechanism, endpoint, and model version confirmed in writing
PRJ-02: Implementation task plan reviewed and approved
INF-01 through INF-04: All Bicep modules deployed and verified in target resource group
All resource names and connection strings available for Core Build wave

waves.name: Core Build
waves.objective: Implement all application logic — TRAPI client, response validator, blob writer, Timer Trigger function, host configuration, and structured logging — producing a locally runnable and unit-testable function.
entry_criteria: TRAPI endpoint, auth scope, and model version confirmed (PRJ-01 complete)
All cloud resources provisioned and RBAC role assignments applied (SEC-01 complete)
exit_criteria: GetAndStoreYankeesRoster function runs locally against real or mocked TRAPI and writes a blob
All structured log events and player_count_returned metric emitted in local test run
host.json updated with functionTimeout=120s and maxConcurrentCalls=1
Prompt template committed to prompts/get_1985_yankees.txt with pinned model version

waves.name: Hardening
waves.objective: Add test coverage, configure observability alerts, complete security audit, and establish the CI/CD pipeline with staging slot to enable safe releases.
entry_criteria: Core Build wave exit criteria met
Function deployable to Azure (staging slot available via INF-05)
exit_criteria: Unit test suite passes with coverage of TRAPI client, validator, and blob writer
Integration test asserts Mattingly, Winfield, and Henderson appear in blob output
All three Application Insights alert rules active (failure, duration, data quality)
Security audit passed — no hardcoded secrets, Key Vault references validated
GitHub Actions CI/CD pipeline triggers on push to main, deploys to staging, runs smoke test

waves.name: Release Readiness
waves.objective: Validate end-to-end in staging, obtain code review approval, complete documentation, and execute the production deployment with all monitoring active.
entry_criteria: CI/CD pipeline operational and smoke test passing in staging
exit_criteria: Nightly timer trigger verified end-to-end in staging (blob written, alerts reachable)
README documents local dev setup, TRAPI auth instructions, and blob naming convention
Production deployment completed via GitHub Actions staging slot swap
All monitoring alerts active in production; first successful nightly run confirmed

tasks.title: Confirm TRAPI auth mechanism, endpoint URL, and GPT-4o model version
tasks.description: Engage the TRAPI team to confirm whether Azure AD Managed Identity bearer tokens are supported, obtain the base URL and API version for the GPT-4o endpoint, and determine the exact model version string to pin. This information gates implementation of the TRAPI client and prompt template.
outputs: Written confirmation of TRAPI auth mechanism (MI or credential type)
TRAPI base URL and API version string
GPT-4o model version string to pin
TRAPI auth scope (if Managed Identity is supported)
acceptance_criteria: TRAPI auth mechanism documented (Managed Identity bearer token scope OR Key Vault credential type)
TRAPI base URL and API version confirmed in writing
GPT-4o model version string identified for pinning in prompt template
Networking requirement confirmed (public internet vs. VNet-restricted)
Player scope confirmed (active roster only vs. extended roster)
risks: TRAPI does not support Managed Identity — requires Key Vault credential and adds Key Vault dependency to critical path
TRAPI is network-restricted — requires VNet integration, adding infrastructure complexity and timeline risk

tasks.title: Review and approve implementation task plan
tasks.description: Stakeholder review and formal approval of this implementation task plan before development begins. Includes a visual dependency graph of all tasks. Automatically approved if the spec designates auto-approval; otherwise requires rciapala sign-off.
outputs: Approved task plan
Confirmed wave sequencing and ownership assignments
acceptance_criteria: All tasks reviewed by rciapala and any designated technical leads
Wave sequencing and critical path confirmed as feasible
Task ownership assignments agreed
Open questions from PRJ-01 incorporated into plan assumptions or risks
Plan approved and development authorized to proceed
risks: Open questions from PRJ-01 unresolved at review time — may require plan revision before approval

tasks.title: Provision dedicated storage account and yankees-roster container via Bicep
tasks.description: Extend or create infra/modules/storage.bicep to provision a dedicated Standard_LRS storage account with Cool access tier, allowSharedKeyAccess:false, soft-delete enabled with 7-day retention, and a private 'yankees-roster' blob container (no anonymous access). This is separate from the Function App host storage account.

yankees-roster container provisioned with correct access settings
Storage account resource ID exported as Bicep output

allowSharedKeyAccess set to false
Blob soft-delete enabled with 7-day retention policy
yankees-roster container exists with publicAccess: None
Storage account is distinct from the Function App host storage account
Bicep deployment succeeds with no errors in target resource group
risks: Naming conflicts with existing storage resources — use uniqueString or parameterized naming

tasks.title: Provision Function App with system-assigned Managed Identity via Bicep
tasks.description: Extend or create infra/modules/functionapp.bicep to provision a Linux Consumption Plan Function App running Python 3.11 with system-assigned Managed Identity enabled. Wire APPLICATIONINSIGHTS_CONNECTION_STRING from INF-04. Host storage account uses identity-based AzureWebJobsStorage__accountName (no listKeys).

tasks.title: Provision Azure Key Vault for TRAPI credentials via Bicep
tasks.description: Provision a zone-redundant Azure Key Vault to store TRAPI credentials (if Managed Identity auth is unsupported) and any other sensitive configuration. Configure Key Vault references in Function App settings. Enable soft-delete and purge protection.

tasks.title: Provision Application Insights and Log Analytics workspace via Bicep
tasks.description: Provision a workspace-based Application Insights resource connected to a Log Analytics workspace with 30-day retention. Enable sampling to control ingestion costs. Wire APPLICATIONINSIGHTS_CONNECTION_STRING to the Function App. This resource is required before INF-02.

tasks.title: Configure RBAC role assignments for Managed Identity via Bicep
tasks.description: Provision all required RBAC role assignments in Bicep: Storage Blob Data Contributor on the yankees-roster container (container-scoped, not account-level), Key Vault Secrets User on the Key Vault, and Storage Blob Data Contributor on the host storage account. Also assign Queue Data Contributor for AzureWebJobsStorage identity-based access.

Managed Identity can read secrets from Key Vault

Bicep deployment succeeds with no errors
risks: Container-scoped RBAC requires the container to exist before assignment — INF-01 must complete first
Propagation delay for new role assignments may cause transient 403 errors in early integration testing

tasks.title: Implement TRAPI client with Managed Identity auth, retry, and timeout
tasks.description: Implement or extend trapi_client.py to authenticate to TRAPI via Managed Identity bearer token (DefaultAzureCredential), send the pinned prompt to the GPT-4o endpoint, enforce a 45-second HTTP timeout, and apply 3 exponential-backoff retries on transient failures. Return the raw response body for downstream validation.
inputs: PRJ-01 outputs (TRAPI endpoint, auth scope, model version)
spec.md — Requirement 1 (TRAPI HTTP call timeout, retry policy)
outputs: trapi_client.py with fetch_roster(prompt_path) function
Handles 45s timeout and 3 exponential-backoff retries
Returns raw response JSON string
acceptance_criteria: DefaultAzureCredential used for Managed Identity token acquisition (no hardcoded keys)
HTTP timeout set to 45 seconds on TRAPI request
3 exponential-backoff retries implemented for transient HTTP errors (5xx, timeout)
GPT-4o model version pinned in request payload
Function accepts prompt file path and returns parsed response body
Unit-testable with mocked HTTP responses
risks: TRAPI auth mechanism change (if PRJ-01 confirms non-MI auth) requires credential injection refactor
TRAPI rate limits or quota may cause retries to exhaust — monitor and adjust backoff parameters

tasks.title: Implement response schema and player count validator
tasks.description: Implement a validator module (response_validator.py or inline in function_app.py) that validates the TRAPI response against the expected schema ({players: [{name, position, jersey_number}]}) and checks that the player count is within the 24–28 range. Returns a typed result (valid/invalid) with error details for downstream routing.
inputs: spec.md — Requirement 1 (Expected Response Schema, player count validation)
PRJ-01 outputs (player scope confirmation — may affect count range)
outputs: validate_response(raw_json) function returning (is_valid: bool, data: dict, error: str)
Schema validation covers required fields and types
Player count check: 24 <= len(players) <= 28
acceptance_criteria: validate_response raises or returns error for missing 'players' key
validate_response raises or returns error if any player is missing name, position, or jersey_number
jersey_number validated as integer type
Player count outside 24–28 range returns is_valid=False with descriptive error
Valid response returns is_valid=True with parsed player list
Unit-testable with fixture data
risks: If player scope is broadened (PRJ-01), count range must be adjusted — defer hardcoding until PRJ-01 confirms scope

tasks.title: Implement blob writer with conditional PUT and failed/ prefix routing
tasks.description: Implement or extend blob_writer.py to write validated roster JSON to yankees-roster/{run_date_utc}.json using DefaultAzureCredential (Managed Identity), with a conditional PUT (If-None-Match: *) to prevent duplicate writes on same-day retrigger. On validation failure, write raw response to yankees-roster/failed/{run_date_utc}.json.
inputs: spec.md — Requirement 2 (blob naming, conditional PUT, failed/ prefix)
INF-01 outputs (storage account name, container name)
outputs: blob_writer.py with write_roster(data, run_date_utc) and write_failed(raw, run_date_utc) functions
Conditional PUT via If-None-Match header prevents duplicate writes
Both success and failure paths write to correct blob paths
acceptance_criteria: DefaultAzureCredential used for BlobServiceClient authentication (no connection string with key)
Blob written to yankees-roster/{run_date_utc}.json on success
Conditional PUT (If-None-Match: *) raises ResourceExistsError on duplicate same-day write (not silently overwritten)
Failed/invalid responses written to yankees-roster/failed/{run_date_utc}.json
Storage account name read from environment variable (not hardcoded)
Unit-testable with mocked BlobServiceClient
risks: RBAC propagation delay may cause 403 on first test run — allow up to 5 minutes after SEC-01 deployment

tasks.title: Implement GetAndStoreYankeesRoster Timer Trigger function
tasks.description: Implement the main Timer Trigger function in function_app.py using the Azure Functions v2 programming model. Configure the timer with schedule '0 0 2 * * *', useMonitor=True, and UTC timezone. Orchestrate the full pipeline: TRAPI fetch → validate → write success blob or failed blob → emit structured log events.
outputs: function_app.py with @app.timer_trigger decorator, schedule='0 0 2 * * *', use_monitor=True
Full pipeline orchestration: fetch → validate → write
Structured log events emitted at each pipeline step
acceptance_criteria: Timer trigger configured with schedule='0 0 2 * * *' and use_monitor=True
UTC timezone explicitly set (not relying on host default)
On TRAPI success and valid response: roster written to success blob path
On TRAPI failure or invalid response: raw response written to failed/ blob path
All five structured log events emitted: function_started, trapi_request_sent, trapi_response_received, blob_write_succeeded, function_completed
player_count_returned custom metric emitted on successful validation
Function completes within 60 seconds under normal TRAPI latency
risks: TRAPI latency approaching 45s may push total execution near functionTimeout — monitor p95 latency

tasks.title: Configure host.json with timeout, concurrency, and sampling settings
tasks.description: Update host.json to set functionTimeout to 120 seconds, maxConcurrentCalls to 1 for the timer trigger, and confirm sampling settings are consistent with spec requirements. Ensure extensionBundle version is compatible with Python 3.11 and Azure Functions v2.
outputs: host.json updated with functionTimeout: 120, extensions.queues.maxDequeueCount aligned, timer maxConcurrentCalls: 1
acceptance_criteria: functionTimeout set to '00:02:00' (120 seconds) in host.json
extensions.queues or timer configuration sets maxConcurrentCalls to 1
samplingSettings.isEnabled remains true with excludedTypes: Request;Exception preserved
extensionBundle version range compatible with Python 3.11 and v2 model
host.json is valid JSON and passes az functionapp validate
risks: Consumption Plan enforces a hard 10-minute function timeout — 120s is well within limit but confirm no plan-level override

tasks.title: Implement structured logging and player_count_returned custom metric
tasks.description: Implement structured logging helpers using OpenTelemetry SDK / azure-monitor-opentelemetry to emit key log events (function_started, trapi_request_sent with model version and prompt hash, trapi_response_received with token count and latency ms, blob_write_succeeded with blob URI, function_completed) and emit the player_count_returned custom metric on each successful run.

tests/test_response_validator.py
tests/test_blob_writer.py
pytest run passing with >80% coverage on target modules

risks: Mocking DefaultAzureCredential and BlobServiceClient requires careful patching — use unittest.mock or pytest-mock

tasks.title: Configure Application Insights alert rules for failure, duration, and data quality
tasks.description: Add Application Insights alert rules via Bicep (infra/modules/alerts.bicep): (1) execution failure alert — fires when failure count > 0 in 1-hour window; (2) duration alert — fires when execution duration > 90 seconds; (3) data quality alert — fires when player_count_returned custom metric < 24 or > 40. All alerts notify rciapala via email Action Group.
outputs: infra/modules/alerts.bicep with three alert rules
Email Action Group targeting rciapala
Alerts deployed and visible in Azure Monitor
acceptance_criteria: Execution failure alert rule fires on function failure count > 0 in 1-hour window
